steps.instruction: Write one sentence that tells the main idea of your paragraph.
steps.explanation: The topic sentence tells the reader what the whole paragraph will be about. Every detail that follows should connect to this idea.

steps.instruction: Write a sentence that gives a reason or fact that supports your topic sentence.
steps.work: Dogs make great pets. They are loyal and love to be with people.
steps.explanation: This detail supports the main idea by explaining one reason dogs are great pets.

steps.instruction: Write one more sentence that gives another reason or example.
steps.work: Dogs make great pets. They are loyal and love to be with people. Dogs can also learn tricks like sit, shake, and roll over.
steps.explanation: A second detail makes the paragraph stronger. Now the reader has two reasons to agree with the topic sentence.

steps.instruction: End the paragraph with a sentence that wraps up the main idea. Do not just repeat the topic sentence.
steps.work: Dogs make great pets. They are loyal and love to be with people. Dogs can also learn tricks like sit, shake, and roll over. That is why so many families choose dogs as their pets.
steps.explanation: The concluding sentence ties everything together. It restates the main idea in a new way without copying the topic sentence word for word.

steps.instruction: Write one sentence that states the main idea.
steps.work: Recycling helps keep our planet clean.
steps.explanation: This topic sentence tells the reader the paragraph will be about how recycling helps the Earth.

steps.instruction: Write a sentence with a fact or reason that supports the main idea.
steps.work: Recycling helps keep our planet clean. When we recycle paper, fewer trees need to be cut down.
steps.explanation: This fact about trees supports the idea that recycling helps the planet.

steps.instruction: Add one more fact or example.
steps.work: Recycling helps keep our planet clean. When we recycle paper, fewer trees need to be cut down. Recycling plastic also keeps trash out of the ocean.
steps.explanation: A second detail gives the reader more evidence. The paragraph is getting stronger.

steps.instruction: Finish the paragraph with a sentence that wraps up the main idea.
steps.work: Recycling helps keep our planet clean. When we recycle paper, fewer trees need to be cut down. Recycling plastic also keeps trash out of the ocean. Everyone can help by putting recyclables in the right bin.
steps.explanation: The concluding sentence wraps up the paragraph and gives the reader something to do. It connects back to the main idea without repeating it.
